raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California landlords currently charge two to three times the monthly rent as a security deposit.",
    "If AB 12 passes, California will become the twelfth state to limit security deposits to one month's rent.",
    "High security deposits can lead renters to borrow from predatory lenders or remain in unsuitable living conditions.",
    "Capping rental deposits could significantly impact housing affordability for families in California.",
    "The average rent for a two-bedroom apartment in San Francisco is $5,000.",
    "A tenant in San Francisco could potentially be asked to pay up to $15,000 to move into an apartment due to security deposit requirements.",
    "Security deposits are already capped at one month's rent in numerous states across the U.S., including New York and Kansas.",
    "The cost of repairs for apartment wear and tear has risen at a rate far lower than the rise in security deposit costs.",
    "Rent in the City of Los Angeles has increased by over 65% in the last decade.",
    "AB 12 will not affect landlords' ability to seek damages from tenants for property harm exceeding the security deposit amount."
  ]
}
```
